skill.name: design-taste-frontend
skill.description: Guides AI agents to build polished React and Next.js frontend interfaces with stronger layout, typography, motion, and component decisions.
skill.summary: Apply senior frontend design taste to AI-generated React and Next.js interfaces.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review shows they are Markdown inline code, CSS examples, and design terminology in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, shell execution, credential access, or data exfiltration were found. The only confirmed minor concern is guidance that may place an external placeholder image URL into generated frontend code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are inline Markdown examples for package names, CSS utilities, React APIs, and animation props. I found no shell execution syntax, script file, or runnable command path in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible Markdown guidance and inline code formatting. There is no code file or execution construct that would run these strings.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Terms Flagged as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are caused by words inside design instructions, headings, and examples. No cryptographic function, scanner, environment probing, or host reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is entirely frontend design guidance. I did not find evidence of cryptographic implementation, system probing, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Placeholder Image URL in Generation Guidance
low_findings.description: SKILL.md recommends a picsum.photos placeholder URL for generated UI assets. This could cause generated applications to load an external image service, but the skill itself does not transmit secrets or execute network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is directly present and could affect generated frontend output. Risk is low because it is static placeholder guidance, not exfiltration code.

content.value_statement: AI-generated frontend work often defaults to generic layouts and weak interaction details. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code strict design rules for polished React and Next.js interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Guides React and Next.js component architecture decisions.
Sets clear rules for typography, spacing, color, and layout variety.
Encourages responsive layouts that collapse safely on mobile screens.
Prompts complete loading, empty, error, hover, and active UI states.
Provides motion guidance for Framer Motion, GSAP, and Three.js use.
Adds frontend performance guardrails for animation and visual effects.
limitations: It is an instruction-only skill and does not include executable tooling.
It does not inspect screenshots or validate visual output by itself.
Some recommendations assume React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, or animation libraries.
Project-specific brand rules can override the default visual preferences.

use_cases.title: Upgrade a SaaS Dashboard
use_cases.description: Use the skill to replace generic card-heavy screens with clearer hierarchy, stronger spacing, and richer interaction states.

use_cases.title: Build a Polished React Prototype
use_cases.description: Use the skill to guide component structure, Tailwind choices, responsive behavior, and animation boundaries.

use_cases.title: Review AI-Generated UI Work
use_cases.description: Use the skill as a checklist for typography, layout, motion, mobile behavior, and frontend performance issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Better Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a React screen for [product area]. Apply the design-taste-frontend rules for layout, typography, color, states, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend page and revise it using the skill rules. Focus on hierarchy, spacing, mobile collapse, interaction states, and visual restraint.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Motion-Rich Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a motion-rich feature section with safe client component boundaries. Use performance-friendly animation choices and avoid generic AI design patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Interface System
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this app interface against the skill rules. Return prioritized fixes for component architecture, density, typography, motion, and production readiness.

output_examples.input: Design a project analytics dashboard for a B2B SaaS app.
output: A left-aligned dashboard layout with restrained color, dense metric groups, and no generic equal-card feature row.
Clear loading, empty, and error states for each data area.
Mobile rules that collapse complex grids into a single readable column.

output_examples.input: Improve a landing page hero that feels too generic.
output: An asymmetric hero direction with controlled type scale and a concrete product visual.
A single accent color, less glow, and stronger spacing rhythm.
Interaction details that support the page without distracting from the offer.

output_examples.input: Add motion to a React feature grid.
output: A client-isolated motion plan using transform and opacity animations.
Spring-based transitions, staggered reveals, and cleanup requirements for heavier effects.
Performance notes for avoiding layout thrash and unnecessary parent renders.

best_practices: Provide the target product, audience, brand tone, and required frontend stack before asking for UI generation.
Ask for responsive behavior, interaction states, and performance constraints in the same prompt.
Verify dependency availability before accepting generated imports or animation library usage.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for accessibility, usability, or browser testing.
Do not force its visual preferences when a project has an established design system.
Do not accept external placeholder assets in production without replacing or approving them.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides AI agents to create more polished frontend interfaces with stronger layout, typography, motion, and state handling.

faq.question: Does it run code or install packages?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown instruction skill and does not include scripts or executable files.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Which frontend stacks fit best?
faq.answer: It is most useful for React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and modern animation libraries.

faq.question: Can project design rules override it?
faq.answer: Yes. Brand guidelines, accessibility requirements, and existing design systems should take priority.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The only confirmed concern is a placeholder image URL that generated output may load from an external service.
